title: Bulking Meal Prep Bowl (High Calorie, High Protein)
metaDescription: Bulking meal prep bowl with 45g of protein and 650 calories per serving — ground beef, rice, black beans and avocado. 5 containers, 35 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: bulking meal prep, muscle building meal prep, high calorie protein bowl
ingredients: 2 cans black beans, drained and rinsed

steps.name: Cook the beef
steps.text: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and taco seasoning, breaking it up as it browns, about 8-10 minutes until fully cooked.

steps.name: Warm the beans
steps.text: Warm the black beans in a small pot or the microwave, seasoning with a pinch of salt.

steps.name: Assemble containers
steps.text: Divide rice, beef, black beans, and cheese evenly between 5 containers.

steps.name: Add avocado fresh
steps.text: Store sliced avocado separately and add it just before eating — it doesn't hold up well pre-sliced in the fridge for days.

tips.question: Why is this recipe higher in calories than the other meal preps on this site?
tips.answer: Bulking means eating in a calorie surplus to support muscle growth, so this recipe is intentionally more calorie-dense than a maintenance or weight-loss meal — that's the entire point of the recipe, not an oversight.

tips.question: Can I make this less calorie-dense if I'm not bulking?
tips.answer: Yes — swap to 93% lean ground beef, cut the cheese in half, or reduce the rice portion, and you'll land closer to 450-500 calories per serving instead of 650.

tips.question: Why store the avocado separately?
tips.answer: Pre-sliced avocado browns and turns mushy within a day or two in the fridge — keeping it whole and slicing fresh right before eating keeps the texture and color much better.

tips.question: How long do these containers keep in the fridge?
tips.answer: Up to 4-5 days for the beef, rice, and beans. Add fresh avocado and reheat only the portion you're eating.

storyIntro: Bulking meal prep has a specific job that's different from every other meal prep on this site: eating enough, not eating light. Ground beef, rice, black beans, and avocado build a bowl that's calorie-dense on purpose.

45g of protein and about 650 calories per container, prepped in 35 minutes across 5 containers — the kind of math that actually supports a calorie surplus instead of accidentally undereating through a busy week.
storyNote: Keep the avocado whole and slice it fresh each day rather than portioning it in ahead of time — pre-sliced avocado sitting in a container for days turns brown and mushy fast, and it's the one ingredient here that doesn't meal-prep well in advance.

images.heroAlt: Meal prep bowl with seasoned ground beef, rice, black beans, sliced avocado and shredded cheese
images.textureAlt: Seasoned ground beef and rice texture with melted cheese and avocado slices
images.pinAlt: Fork lifting seasoned ground beef and rice from a hearty meal prep bowl with avocado
